message:
fix(compress): prevent double-finish from appending spurious gzip/deflate stream

Both GzipCompressor and DeflateCompressor used mem::replace to swap
the encoder for a fresh one in finish(), then called inner.finish()
on the extracted encoder. A second finish() call would extract and
finish the replacement encoder (containing no data), appending a
spurious empty compressed stream (gzip header + empty body + CRC,
or deflate empty block) to the output. Most HTTP clients reject or
misinterpret concatenated compressed streams in Content-Encoding.

Fixed by adding a `finished` bool flag that makes subsequent finish()
calls idempotent no-ops. Regression tests added for both gzip and
deflate.

38 compress tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
